items.label: Setup S-1
items.nl: Observational law on R^2 with centered independent non-Gaussian latent sources (bivariate LvLiNGAM data world) — bypass-justified: Causalean's PO/SCM worlds (POManskiIVSystem, Backdoor, SCM.Do.*, SWIGGraph) are exchangeability/graph-native potential-outcomes abstractions; this world is a linear-non-Gaussian source-mixing law with joint-cumulant coordinates, a strictly different abstraction. Search trace: local_search 'cumulant' -> []; local_search over Causalean/PO/ID/Exact catalogue (ATE/ATT/DID/DTR/LATE/RDD/Frontdoor/Proximal/MultipleInstrumentIV) shows no non-Gaussian-source / ICA / mixing-law world; not found. SURVEYED-AND-REJECTED (bypass-justified, concrete Causalean candidate): Causalean.Discovery.LiNGAM.Kurtosis.cross_fourth_cumulant_eq_sum -- the nearest existing cumulant-identity decl in Causalean. REJECT / abstraction mismatch: it is a fixed SINGLE-ORDER (fourth-order / two-moment, kurtosis) cross-cumulant summation identity over a determinate LiNGAM mixing, whereas this world requires the OVERCOMPLETE ALL-ORDER LvLiNGAM truncation T_L(P) (every joint cumulant order 2<=r<=L=2m+2 simultaneously, with m+2 > 2 latent-plus-idiosyncratic sources and Zariski-generic loadings). The decl's finite-order two-moment scope cannot express the simultaneous binary-form / apolar higher-order structure the maps Phi^b range over; reusing it would silently truncate the observation vector to r=4. Search trace: local_search 'cross_fourth_cumulant_eq_sum' -> [] (sibling-pkg index miss, not absence); confronted via directive-named Causalean.Discovery.LiNGAM.Kurtosis; bypass-justified. Sources S_j as centered real RVs over a common probability space; P as pushforward on R^2; sampling_model = population identification from P (no estimation).

items.label: Setup S-2
items.nl: Complexified structural-parameter and cumulant-coordinate world: affine C-spaces of loadings and source-cumulant weights, the truncated joint-cumulant coordinate space C^{q_L}, and the simultaneous binary-form (divided-power) polynomial maps between them — bypass-justified: no Causalean world models complexified algebraic parameter spaces C^{m+1} x C^{n(L-1)}, Zariski-open genericity loci, polynomial cumulant maps, or their image varieties. Search trace: local_search 'ZariskiClosure' -> [], 'catalecticant' -> []; leansearch/loogle unavailable in this environment; grep of doc/API.md for cumulant|apolar|catalecticant|Zariski|semialgebraic|quantifier|Vandermonde|Waring|LiNGAM -> no matches; not found. Loadings as Fin-indexed C^2 vectors; weights c_{jr},d_{jr} as C-valued families; Phi maps as MvPolynomial-valued simultaneous binary forms indexed by (r,a).

items.label: Definition P-1
items.nl: {P in Laws(R^2) : there exist centered S_0,...,S_{m+1} such that ass:independent-sources, ass:finite-cumulants, ass:source-nongaussianity, ass:forward-axis-model, ass:forward-noncollinearity, and ass:forward-nonzero-edge hold}

items.label: Definition P-2
items.nl: {P in Laws(R^2) : there exist centered S_0,...,S_{m+1} such that ass:independent-sources, ass:finite-cumulants, ass:source-nongaussianity, ass:reverse-axis-model, ass:reverse-noncollinearity, and ass:reverse-nonzero-edge hold}

items.label: Definition P-3
items.nl: T_L(P)=(kappa_{r,a}(P):2<=r<=L,0<=a<=r), where kappa_{r,a}(P)=Cum_P(X repeated r-a times,Y repeated a times) and q_L=L(L+3)/2-2.

items.label: Definition P-4
items.nl: [Phi^right_{m,L}(gamma,rho,c)]_{r,a}=sum_{j=0}^{m+1}c_{jr}(u_{j1})^(r-a)(u_{j2})^a for 2<=r<=L and 0<=a<=r, with u_0=(1,gamma), u_j=(1,rho_j) for 1<=j<=m, and u_{m+1}=(0,1).

items.label: Definition P-5
items.nl: [Phi^left_{m,L}(delta,sigma,d)]_{r,a}=sum_{j=0}^{m+1}d_{jr}(v_{j1})^(r-a)(v_{j2})^a for 2<=r<=L and 0<=a<=r, with v_0=(1,0), v_j=(sigma_j,1) for 1<=j<=m, and v_{m+1}=(delta,1).

items.label: Definition P-7
items.nl: Theta^{right,circ}_{m,L}={(gamma,rho,c) in Theta^right_{m,L}: gamma product_{i=1}^m(gamma-rho_i) product_{1<=i<ell<=m}(rho_i-rho_ell) product_{j=0}^{m+1}product_{r=2}^L c_{jr} !=0}; Theta^{left,circ}_{m,L}={(delta,sigma,d) in Theta^left_{m,L}: delta product_{i=1}^m(delta-sigma_i) product_{1<=i<ell<=m}(sigma_i-sigma_ell) product_{j=0}^{m+1}product_{r=2}^L d_{jr} !=0}.

items.label: Definition P-8
items.nl: R^right_{m,L}(t)={theta in Theta^right_{m,L}: Phi^right_{m,L}(theta)=t} and R^left_{m,L}(t)={eta in Theta^left_{m,L}: Phi^left_{m,L}(eta)=t}.

items.label: Definition P-9
items.nl: For pi in G_m, pi.(gamma,(rho_i)_{i=1}^m,(c_{jr})_{j,r})=(gamma,(rho_{pi(i)})_{i=1}^m,(c_{pi(i),r})_{i=1}^m,c_{0r},c_{m+1,r}); the left action replaces rho,c,gamma by sigma,d,delta. Indices 0 and m+1 are fixed.

items.label: Definition P-10
items.nl: E_m={t in C^{q_K}: [R^right_{m,K}(t) intersects Theta^{right,circ}_{m,K} is nonempty and R^left_{m,K}(t) is nonempty] or [R^left_{m,K}(t) intersects Theta^{left,circ}_{m,K} is nonempty and R^right_{m,K}(t) is nonempty]}; barE_m=ZariskiClosure(E_m); H^right_m=Theta^{right,circ}_{m,K} intersects (Phi^right_{m,K})^{-1}(E_m) and H^left_m=Theta^{left,circ}_{m,K} intersects (Phi^left_{m,K})^{-1}(E_m).

items.label: Definition P-11
items.nl: F^right_{m,L}={(gamma,rho,c) in R^{m+1} x R^{n(L-1)}: gamma!=0, rho_1,...,rho_m,gamma are pairwise distinct, and for every 0<=j<=m+1 there exists a centered non-Gaussian real S_j with E|S_j|^L<infty and Cum(S_j repeated r times)=c_{jr} for every 2<=r<=L}; F^left_{m,L} is the analogous set with delta!=0 and (delta,sigma,d).

items.label: Definition P-12
items.nl: K^star(m)=min{L>=2: for b in {right,left}, outside a proper real algebraic subset of F^b_{m,L}, the value Phi^b_{m,L}(theta) has no representation in F^{opposite(b)}_{m,L}}; set K^star(m)=infinity if the set is empty.

items.label: Definition P-13
items.nl: For b in {right,left}, define a pair. Its first component maps each t in R^{q_K} to the atomic-certificate predicate asserting that there exist b-loading and source-cumulant coordinates lambda and, for each 0<=j<=m+1, witnesses (w_{jh},z_{jh})_{h=1}^{m+2}, such that Phi^b_{m,K}(lambda)=t; the b direct slope is nonzero; its finite loading slopes are pairwise distinct; and, writing k_{jr} for the source cumulants in lambda and mu_{jr}=B_r(0,k_{j2},...,k_{jr}), one has sum_h w_{jh}=1, w_{jh}>=0, sum_h w_{jh}z_{jh}^r=mu_{jr} for 1<=r<=K, and mu_{j2}>0. Its second component is the separate proposition asserting the existence of a real-QE/sign-invariant-CAD decision with variable order t, lambda, then witnesses, together with an apolar rank-open support-annihilator decision certificate for the fixed axis of b. This definition neither proves that proposition nor proves the atomic-certificate predicate equivalent to nonemptiness of the real-source feasible fiber; those require separate decision and realization results.

items.label: Definition P-14
items.nl: Evaluate the two finite formulas FeasFiber^right_m(t) and FeasFiber^left_m(t) from def:global-feasible-fiber-decision. Set S_m(t)=X->Y when the former is true and the latter false; set S_m(t)=Y->X when the latter is true and the former false; and leave S_m(t) undefined otherwise. Thus S_m is a globally finite semialgebraic decision procedure on its declared separated domain, with apolar support-annihilator factorization as its direct generic implementation.

items.label: Definition P-15
items.nl: M^{sep}_{m,K}={M: M has a forward representation with theta in F^right_{m,K} and R^left_{m,K}(T_K(P_M)) intersects F^left_{m,K} is empty, or M has a reverse representation with eta in F^left_{m,K} and R^right_{m,K}(T_K(P_M)) intersects F^right_{m,K} is empty}.

items.label: Definition P-16
items.nl: For m=1 and K=4, A_1 is the 12-equation incidence system t_{r,a}=c_{0r}gamma^a+c_{1r}rho^a+c_{2r}1{a=r}=d_{0r}1{a=0}+d_{1r}sigma^(r-a)+d_{2r}delta^(r-a), for r=2,3,4 and 0<=a<=r, with the generic-locus disjunction; its common-axis subfamily is rho=sigma=0, delta gamma=1, d_{0r}=c_{1r}, d_{1r}=c_{2r}, d_{2r}=c_{0r}gamma^r. For m=2 and K=6, A_2 is the 25-equation system t_{r,a}=c_{0r}gamma^a+c_{1r}rho_1^a+c_{2r}rho_2^a+c_{3r}1{a=r}=d_{0r}1{a=0}+d_{1r}sigma_1^(r-a)+d_{2r}sigma_2^(r-a)+d_{3r}delta^(r-a), for r=2,...,6 and 0<=a<=r, with the generic-locus disjunction; its common-axis subfamily is rho_1=sigma_1=0, delta gamma=1, sigma_2 rho_2=1, d_{0r}=c_{1r}, d_{1r}=c_{3r}, d_{2r}=c_{2r}rho_2^r, d_{3r}=c_{0r}gamma^r.

items.label: Definition P-17
items.nl: Compare the simultaneous binary-form decompositions Phi^right_{m,K}(theta)=Phi^left_{m,K}(eta) after quotienting each same-arrow fiber by G_m, using the axis directions fixed in the two maps and the Jacobian ranks of the resulting quotient-fiber equations.

items.label: Definition P-18
items.nl: Define the proposition that there exist forward and reverse real parameters theta and eta, each belonging to the compactly-supported feasible region through K_-=2m+1, whose forward and reverse truncated cumulant maps agree through K_-. The definition asserts no construction or proof of this existence proposition.

items.label: Definition P-19
items.nl: EXTERNAL (CITED) CLASSICAL INTERFACE, NOT PROVED HERE: for every finite family of real polynomials and variable order, there exists an adapted cylindrical algebraic decomposition with sign-invariance for the generated projection family, recursive section/sector lifting, cellwise decision of polynomial sign conditions, and Tarski-Seidenberg semialgebraic projection. Sources: BochnakCosteRoy1998 Section 2.3 and Theorem 2.2.1; BasuPollackRoy2006 Chapter 5. The interface is paper-agnostic, purely existence-and-structure, and carries no algorithm, computability, payload, or complexity claim. It remains distinct from def:effective-rational-groebner-cad-interface.

items.label: Definition P-20
items.nl: EXTERNAL (CITED) GENERAL INTERFACE, NOT PROVED HERE: there exist natural constants a >= 1 and b and fixed uniform exact symbolic machines which, for finite supplied polynomial presentations over Q and Q(i), supplied admissible monomial and variable orders, and supplied retained blocks, terminate with certified Groebner bases, normal forms, elimination ideals, ideal intersections, saturations, the rational complex elimination-closure theorem, and a sign-invariant real CAD with exact root/sign data, recursively lifted section/sector cells, quantifier elimination, cellwise truth/retention, and finite certified traces. If D >= 1, every job has dimension at most N and supplied degree envelope at most D, and the total supplied source-polynomial count is at most s, one combined certificate bounds the sum of a finite batch of rational algebra traces, a finite batch of Gaussian-rational algebra traces, and the rational CAD/QE trace by (max 2 (sD))^(2^(aN+b)). The same fixed machines and constants certify the dependent saturated-elimination/intersection/CAD pipeline when D also bounds its actual intermediate bases and CAD input. The interface is paper-agnostic and assumes no LiNGAM map, moment certificate, exceptional locus, fiber, atlas cell, label, or conclusion.

items.label: Assumption A-1
items.nl: S_0 perp S_1 perp ... perp S_{m+1}

items.label: Assumption A-3
items.nl: for every 0<=j<=m+1, S_j is not Gaussian

items.label: Lemma L-1
items.nl: For each pi in G_m, Phi^right_{m,L}(pi.theta)=Phi^right_{m,L}(theta) and Phi^left_{m,L}(pi.eta)=Phi^left_{m,L}(eta). If a forward structural model M has real-feasible parameter theta, relabelling its latent sources by pi gives a forward structural model with the same observational law P_M and D(M)=X->Y; the analogous reverse statement has D(M)=Y->X. Hence quotienting a same-arrow fiber by G_m identifies only source labels and never identifies opposite arrows.

items.label: Theorem T-1
items.nl: Put n=m+2 and K=2n-2. There are Zariski-open dense sets U^right_m subset Theta^{right,circ}_{m,K} and U^left_m subset Theta^{left,circ}_{m,K}, each meeting its real feasible region in a nonempty relatively Euclidean-open set, such that R^left_{m,K}(Phi^right_{m,K}(theta)) is empty for every theta in U^right_m and R^right_{m,K}(Phi^left_{m,K}(eta)) is empty for every eta in U^left_m. More explicitly, from t=T_K(P) in either set one forms the divided-power blocks f_r(x,y)=sum_{a=0}^r binom(r,a)t_{r,a}x^(r-a)y^a; the common kernel of q |-> (q(partial)f_{n+k})_{0<=k<=n-2} is the line generated by the squarefree degree-n support annihilator Q_D=product_{ell in D}ell^perp. Factoring Q_D recovers the unordered loading-direction set D, and its vertical-versus-horizontal fixed axis decides the arrow against every full opposite-arrow fiber.

items.label: Theorem T-2
items.nl: For every m >= 1 at K = 2m+2, there are Zariski-open dense loci meeting the real feasible regions on which the loading direction is recovered and no full opposite-arrow fiber exists. However, the same-arrow fiber is not a single G_m-orbit: an omitted source-pair permutation already gives multiple orbits, and for m >= 2 every generic fiber has dimension m(m-1)/2 from the low-order weight kernels.

items.label: Theorem T-3
items.nl: For every m >= 1 at K = 2m+2, the complex exceptional closure barE_m has codimension one in each arrow-image variety, not codimension m when m >= 2. Its two generic parameter preimages are exactly the points retaining a full opposite-arrow representation, and the full-fiber polynomial incidence equations specialize explicitly at m = 1, K = 4 and m = 2, K = 6.

items.label: Theorem T-4
items.nl: For every m >= 3, generic real arrow recovery already holds from cumulants through order 2m+1, so K^star(m) <= 2m+1. Consequently the proposed identity K^star(m) = 2m+2 for every m >= 1 is false.

items.label: Remark T-5
items.nl: For every fixed m, there is a finite computable semialgebraic cylindrical atlas of the real exceptional closure such that, on each base cell, the complete feasible forward and reverse fibers are finite unions of recursively cylindrical section and sector cells and their nonemptiness labels are constant. The atlas includes singular and nongeneric boundary branches and decides exactly when both real arrows are feasible.

items.label: G_m
items.nl: realized by admissibleSourceSwap, Arrow, admissibleSourceSwapArrow, admissibleSourceSwapTagged

items.label: pi
items.nl: realized by admissibleSourceSwap, Arrow, admissibleSourceSwapArrow, admissibleSourceSwapTagged

items.label: b
items.nl: realized by admissibleSourceSwap, Arrow, admissibleSourceSwapArrow, admissibleSourceSwapTagged

items.label: E_m
items.nl: realized by workedCompatibilitySystems, genericFullFiberCompatibility, genericCompatibilityClosure, genericCompatibilityPreimage, genericFullFiberCompatibilityLocus

items.label: barE_m
items.nl: realized by genericFullFiberCompatibility, genericCompatibilityClosure, genericCompatibilityPreimage, genericFullFiberCompatibilityLocus

items.label: H^right_m
items.nl: realized by genericFullFiberCompatibility, genericCompatibilityClosure, genericCompatibilityPreimage, genericFullFiberCompatibilityLocus

items.label: H^left_m
items.nl: realized by genericFullFiberCompatibility, genericCompatibilityClosure, genericCompatibilityPreimage, genericFullFiberCompatibilityLocus

items.label: S_m
items.nl: realized by feasibleFiberFormula, feasibleFiberDecision, directionSelector, directionSelectorWithDecision
